notes: Server-internal disk storage; one of four storage architecture categories probed in interview guide.
External disk enclosures directly attached to servers; distinct from SAN/NAS in interview taxonomy.
Shared block storage fabric; one of four architecture categories. [FROM CACHE]
File-level network storage; one of four architecture categories. [FROM CACHE]
High-performance spinning disk; one of three disk technology types in interview taxonomy (SCSI / FC / ATA).
High-performance disk interface for SAN; one of three disk tech types probed. [FROM CACHE]
Proposed midline disk technology for MaXLine; subject of study. ATA later superseded by Serial ATA (SATA) after 2003; midline SATA became mainstream enterprise tier.
Next-generation ATA interface; emerging at time of study; became dominant midline/nearline enterprise tier post-2005. [FROM CACHE]
Parity-based redundancy; listed in guide's data protection question as primary redundancy method.
Mirror-based redundancy; listed alongside parity RAID in data protection taxonomy.
Synchronous/asynchronous disaster recovery replication; listed in data protection question.
Both indexed and full-volume point-in-time copies probed; forerunner of modern snapshot/clone technologies.
Tape libraries with automated loaders; probed as the lowest tier of the storage hierarchy below disk.
Traditional update-intensive database workload; first application category in interview classification framework.
Cited as exemplar of Contemporary OLTP (mixed read/write). SAP later evolved into S/4HANA.
BI/query-intensive sequential-read workload; one of eight application categories. [FROM CACHE]
Personal productivity file type; classified as write-once/read-few; cited as key candidate for midline storage migration.
Large-file mixed read/write application category in classification taxonomy.
